page: Microsoft Azure L80as_v3 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the LASv3 family. It features 80 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD processors and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 640.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server configuration includes 20019 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, facilitating high-capacity local data operations. It does not include any graphics processing units and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L80as_v3 offers high resource density, combining a large memory allocation with substantial local storage capacity on a single node. The primary tradeoff is the lack of hardware-accelerated graphics processing and the absence of complimentary public IPv4 addresses, which must be provisioned separately if external connectivity is required.

Due to its high local storage capacity and memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server is designed for storage-intensive and memory-intensive workloads. Suitable applications include large-scale databases, big data analytics, enterprise applications, and data warehousing.
description: Microsoft Azure L80as_v3 is an x86_64 architecture server within the LASv3 family, utilizing AMD processors and running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 80 dedicated vCPUs and 640.0 GB of system memory, establishing an 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio. A key characteristic of this server is its substantial 20019 GB of local storage, which is Premium Storage capable, making it highly suitable for data-intensive operations. The instance does not include any GPUs and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, the L80as_v3 provides high resource density by pairing massive local storage with a large memory footprint, though it lacks hardware acceleration. This configuration is optimized for large-scale databases, data warehousing, big data analytics, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

tagline: An AMD-powered server featuring eighty dedicated virtual cores, substantial memory capacity, and over twenty terabytes of high-capacity local storage.

640.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
